content.text:
Physics Lab Sessions 2 – Electricity
Semester:
2  |
Teaching Unit:
Methodology Unit (MU)
Course Description
This laboratory course consolidates the theoretical knowledge acquired in the Electricity module (Physics 2) and develops practical skills in experimental techniques. Students will learn how to set up, operate, and interpret measurements in various electrical experiments, enabling them to visualize and better understand the phenomena studied in lectures and tutorials.
Learning Outcomes
Perform accurate measurements of electric fields and potentials using laboratory instruments.
Construct and analyze basic electrical circuits, applying Ohm's law and measuring resistance.
Use the Wheatstone bridge method for precise resistance measurement.
Operate oscilloscopes and signal generators, including transformer-based systems.
Measure and analyze the behavior of capacitors, including series/parallel combinations and charge–discharge cycles.
Verify the Biot–Savart law experimentally.
Determine the Earth's magnetic field using appropriate apparatus.
Lab Schedule and Topics
Measurement of electric field and potential (rheographic tank)
Electrical circuits: Ohm's law, resistor combinations, resistance measurements
Wheatstone bridge
Oscilloscope and current generator (transformer)
Capacitors: combinations, capacitance measurement, charge–discharge experiments
Verification of Biot–Savart law
Measurement of Earth's magnetic field
Assessment Method
Continuous assessment: 50%
Final exam: 50%
References
Caubarrere, J. L. – Electricity and Waves: Lectures and Lab Sessions. OPU, Algiers (1986).
Bentounsi, A. – General Electricity, Vol. 2: Solved Exercises. OPU, Algiers (1992).
Ediscience Collective – University Physics: Electrostatics and Direct-Current Circuits, Years 1 and 2. Ediscience International (2010).
Fedullo, D., Gallauziaux, T. – Electricity: How to Build Your Own Electrical Installation. Eyrolles (2012).
Largeaud, H. – Electrical Schematics. Eyrolles (2006).
